expected_behavior: The eval module registry parses through @krn/contracts, contains module descriptors outside product code, and rejects duplicate module IDs.
failure_mode: The eval module list moves back into product code or accepts duplicate module IDs that make lane selection ambiguous.

expected_behavior: The valid krn-eval report fixture parses through @krn/contracts, declares default current lane selection, includes core/current lanes, and excludes lab.
failure_mode: The krn eval aggregate contract can no longer validate its default current-lane example.

expected_behavior: The known-bad fixture missing required lane/caveat fields fails deterministically.
failure_mode: The validator accepts an aggregate eval report that can be overclaimed without the lane/caveat contract.

expected_behavior: A report that includes a lab module while lane_selection excludes lab fails deterministically.
failure_mode: The contract allows historical lab modules to appear inside the default current-lane report.

expected_behavior: The CLI writes an aggregate eval report under .krn/eval, the generated report parses through @krn/contracts, the default current lane includes core/current modules, and lab modules are excluded by default.
failure_mode: The krn eval command keeps running historical lab modules by default or stops producing a schema-backed aggregate runtime artifact.

expected_behavior: Explicit --module selection writes a custom lane report and can run a lab module only when directly requested.
assertions: custom module keeps lab lane explicit
failure_mode: Focused module execution is broken, or lab modules can only be reached through the default eval path.
